children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is established within the Coast Guard a Western Alaska Oil Spill Planning Criteria Program (referred to in this section as the “Program”) to develop and administer the Western Alaska oil spill planning criteria.

children.label: A
children.text: the primary duty of whom shall be to administer the Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: who shall not be subject to frequent or routine reassignment.

children.heading: .—Conflicts of interest
children.label: 2 .—Conflicts of interest
children.text: The individual selected to serve as the Program Manager shall not have conflicts of interest relating to entities regulated by the Coast Guard.

children.label: i
children.text: approval, drills, and testing relating to the Western Alaska oil spill planning criteria; and

children.label: ii
children.text: gathering input concerning such planning criteria from Federal agencies, State and local governments, Tribes, and relevant industry and nongovernmental entities.

children.label: i
children.text: assess whether such existing planning criteria adequately meet the needs of vessels operating in the geographic area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: identify methods for advancing response capability so as to achieve, with respect to a vessel, compliance with national planning criteria.

children.heading: .—Onsite verifications
children.label: C .—Onsite verifications
children.text: The Program Manager shall address the relatively small number and limited nature of verifications of response capabilities for vessel response plans by increasing, within the Coast Guard Arctic District, the quantity and frequency of onsite verifications of the providers identified in vessel response plans.

children.label: i
children.text: the national planning criteria described in part 155 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of enactment of this section);

children.label: ii
children.text: alternative planning criteria;

children.label: iii
children.text: Western Alaska oil spill planning criteria;

children.label: iv
children.text: Captain of the Port and Federal On-Scene Coordinator authorities related to activation of a vessel response plan;

children.label: v
children.text: the responsibilities of vessel owners and operators in preparing a vessel response plan for submission; and

children.label: vi
children.text: responsibilities of the Area Committee, including risk analysis, response capability, and development of alternative planning criteria;

children.label: B
children.text: explains the approval processes of vessel response plans that involve alternative planning criteria or Western Alaska oil spill planning criteria; and

children.label: i
children.text: in any geographic area in the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: specifically in the Coast Guard Arctic District; and
providing such training to all Coast Guard personnel involved in the Program.

children.heading: .—Alternative planning criteria
children.label: 1 .—Alternative planning criteria
children.text: The term “alternative planning criteria” means criteria submitted under section 155.1065 or 155.5067 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of enactment of this section), for vessel response plans.

children.heading: .—Tribe
children.label: 2 .—Tribe
children.text: The term “Tribe” has the meaning given the term “Indian Tribe” in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().25 U.S.C. 5304

children.heading: .—Vessel response plan
children.label: 3 .—Vessel response plan
children.text: The term “vessel response plan” means a plan required to be submitted by the owner or operator of a tank vessel or a nontank vessel under regulations issued by the President under section 311(j)(5) of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ().33 U.S.C. 1321(j)(5)

children.heading: .—Western alaska oil spill planning criteria
children.label: 4 .—Western alaska oil spill planning criteria
children.text: The term “Western Alaska oil spill planning criteria” means the criteria required to be established under paragraph (9) of section 311(j) of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ().33 U.S.C. 1321(j)
